title: crotalaria pleiophylla
intro: Crotalaria pleiophylla, also known as the 'many-leaved rattlebox', is a plant species in the legume family, characterized by its distinctive clusters of small, yellow flowers and compound leaves. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its seed pods which contain a toxic compound called pyrrolizidine alkaloids, however, in small quantities, it has been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Crotalaria pleiophylla include its traditional use in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and seeds are often used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, although it is essential to exercise caution and consult with a healthcare professional before using this plant due to its potential toxicity.
